name: Mahatma Gandhi
key_contributions: Non-violence, Satyagraha, Salt March, Quit India
text: Independence: Mahatma Gandhi (1869-1948). Father of the Nation. Pioneer of non-violence and Satyagraha. Led Salt March (1930), Quit India Movement (1942), Non-Cooperation Movement (1920). Assassinated January 30, 1948. His principles shaped India's democratic foundation.

name: Jawaharlal Nehru
key_contributions: Discovery of India, First PM, modern India architect
text: Independence: Jawaharlal Nehru (1889-1964). First Prime Minister. Key role in independence struggle. Authored Discovery of India. 'Tryst with Destiny' speech at midnight Aug 15, 1947. Shaped modern India's democratic, secular, and scientific foundations.

name: Subhas Chandra Bose
key_contributions: Indian National Army, Azad Hind Fauj, Forward Bloc
text: Independence: Subhas Chandra Bose (1897-1945). Founded Indian National Army (Azad Hind Fauj). 'Give me blood, I shall give you freedom.' Formed Azad Hind government. INA marched toward India through Burma. Netaji's military approach complemented Gandhian non-violence.

name: B.R. Ambedkar
key_contributions: Constitution drafting, Dalit rights, social justice
text: Independence: B.R. Ambedkar (1891-1956). Chairman of Constitution Drafting Committee. Father of the Indian Constitution. Champion of Dalit rights and social justice. First Law Minister. Bharat Ratna 1990 (posthumous). Anti-caste movement leader.

name: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
role: Iron Man of India, First Home Minister
key_contributions: Integration of princely states, Statue of Unity
text: Independence: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel (1875-1950). Iron Man of India. Unified 562 princely states into Indian Union. First Home Minister and Deputy PM. Statue of Unity (182m, world's tallest) in Gujarat. Bharat Ratna 1991.

name: Bhagat Singh
key_contributions: Armed resistance, Assembly bomb, Lahore Conspiracy
text: Independence: Bhagat Singh (1907-1931). Revolutionary freedom fighter. Threw bombs in Central Legislative Assembly (1929) as protest. Lahore Conspiracy Case. Hanged at age 23 on March 23, 1931. 'Inquilab Zindabad' became iconic slogan.

name: Sarojini Naidu
role: Nightingale of India, First Woman Governor
key_contributions: Poet, first woman governor, women's rights
text: Independence: Sarojini Naidu (1879-1949). Nightingale of India. First woman president of Indian National Congress (1925). First woman Governor of a state (UP, 1947). Renowned poet. Active in Salt March and Quit India Movement.

name: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad
key_contributions: INC President, education system architect, communal harmony
text: Independence: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad (1888-1958). First Education Minister of India. Youngest INC President at 35. Established IITs, UGC, and AICTE framework. Championed communal harmony and Hindu-Muslim unity. Bharat Ratna 1992.
